title: centaurea oriolii-bolosii
intro: Centaurea oriolii-bolosii, also known as the Bolos' Knapweed, is a rare species of flowering plant in the daisy family. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids. The health benefits associated with Centaurea oriolii-bolosii include reducing inflammation, alleviating pain, and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of Centaurea oriolii-bolosii are typically made by infusing the dried flowers and leaves in hot water to create a tea, or by using the plant's extracts in capsules or tinctures.
